title: Erythroseris somalensis
intro: Erythroseris somalensis, commonly known as Somali Spilanthes, is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Erythroseris somalensis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional medicine preparations, such as syrups and tinctures.
